title: Summer School
text:
NEMD for Fluids and Interfaces · 6–7 July 2026 · University of Edinburgh
The first NEMD summer school ran over the two days before NEMD 2026, with 39 participants. Ten lectures covered NEMD theory and methods for fluids and interfaces, paired with hands-on sessions running LAMMPS on the Cirrus supercomputer with EPCC support.

School website: nemd-school.org
Hands-on materials (LAMMPS + Python, open to all): github.com/Non-Equilibrium-Molecular-Dynamics/NEMD-Summer-School-2026


The 2026 summer school at the Nucleus, King's Buildings.

title: NEMD 2025, Osaka
text:
4th conference · 11–13 June 2025 · Osaka University Nakanoshima Center
The fourth meeting and the first outside the UK. Chaired by Yasutaka Yamaguchi (Osaka University), with vice chairs Takeshi Omori and Takuya Kuwahara (Osaka Metropolitan University), the meeting was supported by the Japan Society for the Promotion of Science as a UK-Japan collaborative initiative, with refreshments sponsored by Tokyo Electron.
More detail is on the NEMD 2025 conference site.

NEMD 2025 at Osaka University Nakanoshima Center.

title: NEMD 2021, online
text:

The second meeting of the series was held online during the pandemic, over Microsoft Teams. The full sessions were recorded and remain available:

title: Conferences
text:
The NEMD conference series has met five times since 2020, in the UK, online, and in Japan. Each meeting has its own page with dates, organisers, and materials.

  
    
    2026 · EdinburghNEMD 2026The 5th and largest meeting yet: 5 keynotes, 24 talks, 15 posters, 76 delegates from 15 countries, and the first summer school.
  
  
    
    2025 · OsakaNEMD 2025The 4th meeting and the first outside the UK, hosted at Osaka University Nakanoshima Center.
  
  
    
    2024 · LondonNEMD 2024The 3rd meeting: two days of talks and poster sessions at Imperial College London.
  
  
    2nd
    2021 · OnlineNEMD 2021The online meeting, with full session recordings available on YouTube.
  
  
    1st
    2020 · LondonNEMD 2020The kick-off meeting at Brunel University London that started the series.

title: About
text: Non-equilibrium molecular dynamics (NEMD) is the study of fundamental fluid flow using molecular simulation: non-equilibrium because the system is driven away from thermodynamic equilibrium by wall motion, temperature and pressure gradients, or because it contains an interface such as a liquid-vapour coexistence.

title: About
text:
The community
NEMD started in 2020 as a special interest group of the UK Fluids Network, kicked off at a meeting at Brunel University London on 17 January 2020. Since then it has grown into an international conference series: five meetings across the UK, online, and Japan, with the largest, NEMD 2026 in Edinburgh, gathering 76 delegates from 15 countries alongside the first NEMD summer school.
The name has a longer history: NEMD workshops ran in the 2010s before the current series, and the field’s roots go back to the first molecular simulations of shear flow. A fuller history page is planned; if you have material from the earlier era, please get in touch.

title: About
text:
Get involved

Join the mailing list: email contact.nemd@gmail.com with the subject line “subscribe”.
A seminar series is planned; announcements will go to the mailing list first.

title: About
text:
Contact
Email contact.nemd@gmail.com, or reach the SIG through the UK Fluids Network page.

title: NEMD 2020, London
text:
1st meeting · 17 January 2020 · Brunel University London
The meeting that started the series. Organised by Edward Smith (Brunel University London) and James Ewen (Imperial College London) as the kick-off of the Non-Equilibrium Molecular Dynamics special interest group of the UK Fluids Network.
Records of this first meeting are sparse. If you have photos or a programme from the day, please email contact.nemd@gmail.com; we would like to complete the archive.

title: NEMD 2024, London
text:
3rd conference · 13–14 June 2024 · Imperial College London
The third meeting: two days of talks and poster sessions at Imperial College London, with a conference dinner.
The book of abstracts is available from the UK Fluids Network.

NEMD 2024 at Imperial College London.

title: NEMD 2026, Edinburgh
text:
5th conference · 8–10 July 2026 · The Nucleus, University of Edinburgh
The fifth meeting was the largest yet: 76 delegates from 15 countries across 4 continents, with 5 keynotes, 24 talks in total, and 15 posters presented through lightning-talk sessions. It was also the first meeting with a summer school, held on the two days before the conference.
For the full timetable, see the conference programme (PDF).
NEMD 2026 delegates at the Nucleus, King's Buildings.

title: NEMD 2026, Edinburgh
text:
Best Poster Award
Voted by the delegates, the award was shared by two posters:

Tiziano Cavalieri (University of Edinburgh), “A Non-Equilibrium Approach to Molecular Modelling of Gas Transport in a Mixed Matrix Membrane”
Taher Meydando (University of Edinburgh), “Determination of Nanobubble Zeta Potential via Non-Equilibrium Molecular Dynamics”


Poster prize winners Tiziano Cavalieri and Taher Meydando with committee member Eleonora Ricci.

title: NEMD 2026, Edinburgh
text:
Organisation
Organised by Rohit Pillai (chair), Duncan Dockar, Eleonora Ricci, and Santiago Romero-Vargas Castrillón (University of Edinburgh), Saikat Datta (Swansea University), and Edward Smith (Brunel University London).
Sponsored by CCP5, Tokyo Electron Ltd., EPCC, Brunel University London, Swansea University, and the Multiscale Flow X group.
